50.reason: This increments i for every word found, but the logic for adding new words is flawed; i should only increment when a new word is actually added, not for duplicates.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  This increments i for every word found, but the logic for adding new words is flawed; i should only increment when a new word is actually added, not for duplicates.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The increment of i should only happen when a new word is added, not every time a word is read. This causes array indices to be skipped and incorrect counts.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: i is incremented unconditionally after the for loop. If a word is found and break occurs, i still increases, causing i to be larger than the actual number of unique words, leading to uninitialized entries in later processing. Also, when i==0, the for loop doesn't execute, and i++ makes i=1 without adding the first word.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This line increments 'i' regardless of whether a new word was added or an existing one was found. reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Error] 'i' is incremented for every word processed, not just unique words. This causes 'qsort' and the output loop to process uninitialized array elements. It should only increment when a new word is added.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [i is incremented regardless of whether the word was new or existing, causing i to overcount and leading to printing of zero-count or uninitialized words.]
